title: uecreate
text: uecreate Initialize the Uniface runtime for component call-in. Explain this code long uecreate (intrunMode,UVOID*hInstance,USTRING*cmdLine,USTRING*asnName,USTRING*apsName,USTRING*workDir, UHACT*phEnv); Copy Parameters runMode Specifies how Uniface is to be initialized (in batch mode or interactive mode). Currently, only batch mode is supported, so you should always set this parameter to 1. hInstance Obsolete. Always set it to 0 (zero) cmdLine String specifying Uniface command line options, such as /asn= or /log . Set this to zero or to an empty string if no command line options are needed. asnName Name of the assignment file to be used during initialization. This file name can be either fully qualified (recommended) or relative to the working directory as specified by the workDir parameter. If you do want to use a specific assignment file, specify zero or an empty string. In this case, uecreate will read usysadm:usys.asn (as with a normal Uniface application) and it will attempt to read the special assignment file uni3gl.asn in the working directory (if present). apsName Name of an application shell ( .aps ) file name. This parameter is currently ignored, so you should set it to zero or to an empty string. This means that it is not possible to let uecreate do any application-level initialization (which you would normally do in the apStart trigger of the application shell) nor set up an application-level apStart trigger. workDir Working directory. Use zero or an empty string if you do not want to set a working directory. In this case, the working directory will be left unchanged from what it was when you started your program. phEnv Uniface environment handle. This handle can be used later to shut down the environment with uedelete . (Output) Note: It is possible for pathnames supplied to this function to contain spaces, and if they do, you need to quote them. For example: cmdLine="/asn=/"my long pathname//myasn.asn/" /log=/"my other long pathname//log.txt/"" Return Values Table 1. Values returned by uecreate Value Error constant Meaning 1 UINI_SUCCESS The Uniface environment was successfully initialized. -1 UINI_LOAD_ERROR 4GL initialization failed. -2 UINI_LICENSE_ERROR You specified runMode zero, which is not allowed. -4 UINI_ALLOC_ERROR Memory allocation error We recommend that you use the mnemonic values rather than the numeric values. For example, you should test for UINI_SUCCESS rather than for 1. While this may make your code slightly harder to debug, it improves readability and portability. In practice, uecreate will almost never return a negative code. In most cases, it will either return UINI_SUCCESS or it will not return at all. When there is a run-time error, such as an assignment statement error or a license error, the Uniface runtime performs a fatal exit within uecreate , so control is not returned to the calling program. Use Library: ucall Description uecreate initializes the Uniface environment for component call-in. If you want to call Uniface components from user code, calling uecreate is the first thing you should do (unless your user code was called from Uniface, in which case the environment is already initialized). The handle returned by uecreate can be used to create component instances. Alternatively, you can call ueopen to get a fresh copy of the environment handle, and use that handle for instantiating components. This is what generated call-in stubs do, because they cannot assume that uecreate has been called. An important part of the work uecreate does is to load up all the Uniface shared libraries. You must make sure that you have correctly set the environment variable that specifies the search path for shared libraries, to avoid loading shared libraries that belong to another Uniface version. For more information, see the "Loading Shared Libraries" topic. . Example: uecreate, uedelete, ufreeh, uinstdel, uinstnew, uinstopr, and uopract The following simple call-in program shows how to activate the EXEC operation of the service MYSVC. As usual, error checking has been omitted for the sake of brevity. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> void main (void) { UHACT hEnv; /* Uniface environment */ UHACT hIn; /* Instance handle */ UHACT hOp; /* Operation handle */ long opStat; /* Activate status return parameter */ /* Create the environment */ uecreate(1, 0, 0, "my.asn", 0, 0, &hEnv); /* Instantiate service MYSVC */ uinstnew(hEnv, "MYSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, 0); uinstopr(hIn, "EXEC", 0, &hOp); /* Instantiate the EXEC operation */ uopract (hOp, &opStat, 0); /* Activate the operation */ uinstdel(hIn, 0); /* Destroy the instance */ ufreeh (&hOp); /* Free the operation */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free the instance */ uedelete(hEnv, -1); /* Shut down the environment */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free the environment handle */ return 0; // on success } Copy Related Links Loading Shared Libraries Shared libraries can be loaded statically at startup or dynamically at runtime. uedelete Close down the Uniface environment that was created by uecreate. ueopen Return the current Uniface environment handle that can be used to instantiate services.
